strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience with TypeScript, Node.js, and React directly matching the primary stack across multiple production roles
Active AI agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) — the one résumé that mentions this required competency directly
Deep testing culture: TDD practitioner, authored Fluent-Asserts and Trial test libraries, ran multi-day TDD/Selenium workshops, applied CI/CD and trunk-based dev at RIDE Capital
concerns: NestJS is not mentioned anywhere in the résumé — the primary backend framework for this role is entirely absent
Kotlin/Java experience is limited and dated: Java appears only briefly at Altom (2016–2018) for robotic arms; no Kotlin mentioned at all
No explicit mention of Jest, Vitest, or Playwright — testing expertise is demonstrated through D-language tooling and methodology, not the required JS/TS testing frameworks
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong fullstack engineer whose TypeScript, Node.js, and React experience maps directly onto the primary stack, and his current work on MCP-based AI agent tooling at re:cinq is concrete evidence for the one competency many candidates lack entirely. His testing philosophy is well-established (TDD workshops, custom test libraries, CI/CD discipline), and cloud/container coverage is solid (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes). The meaningful gap is NestJS — the declared primary backend framework — which goes unmentioned, suggesting either no production exposure or an oversight; this warrants direct probing. Kotlin/Java depth is thin, but those are secondary. The score reflects a high baseline with one notable framework blind spot.
